t_ch: 舱门开启，人偶冰冷的手指将你从中拉了出来，新鲜的空气争先恐后地涌入鼻腔。
t_en: A hatch opens, and the cold fingers of a puppet pull you out from within. Fresh air surges into your nostrils.

t_ch: 老实点，不碰它。
t_en: Behave yourself. Don't touch it.

t_ch: 为了维护星际间难能可贵的和平，你决定还是别乱摸了，以免生出什么事端来。
t_en: To maintain the precious order of intergalactic peace, you decide not to mess with this pod in case anything bad happens.

t_ch: 连声谢谢都没有吗？
t_en: Not even a word of thanks?

t_ch: 谢谢你，黑塔女士。
t_en: Thank you, Madam Herta.

t_ch: 那还不快去测试模拟宇宙？
t_en: Then why don't you run a few quick tests inside the Simulated Universe?

t_ch: （向她投诉设备的安全问题）
t_en: (Complain to her about the safety of the facilities)

t_ch: 你跟艾丝妲说啊，别找我。
t_en: Tell Asta, I don't want to know about it.

t_ch: 至于这个罐头…有趣，原来阮•梅在捣鼓这个——
t_en: As for this can... Interesting... So Ruan Mei has been tinkering with this...

t_ch: 捣鼓什么？
t_en: Tinkering with what?

t_ch: 你怎么不说话了？
t_en: Cat got your tongue?

t_ch: 人偶没有回应，看来黑塔还有别的事要忙，先行下线了。
t_en: The puppet gives no response. Seems like Herta is busy with other stuff and had to go offline.

t_ch: 极其普通的杂物箱，你不知道是哪位无聊人士把它们堆叠在一起的。堆叠的手法并不严谨，箱体看起来有些摇摇欲坠，你决定——
t_en: Ordinary boxes filled with clutter. You have no idea who was bored enough to stack them all up together like this. They aren't stacked very neatly, and seem to be on the verge of toppling over. You decide to...

t_ch: 做好事，扶一把。
t_en: Be a good person and stabilize them.

t_ch: 你向来心善，如果视野内的物品头顶挂着感叹号，无论大事小事，你都会仗义出手。
t_en: You've always been kind. No matter how big or small the job is, if you ever see an exclamation mark hanging over the top of something, you'll always take action.

t_ch: 尽管这里没有什么感叹号，连问号也没有，但你还是决定行动起来，谁让你天生一副热心肠呢。
t_en: Despite there being no exclamation marks here — nor a single question mark, either — you decide to take action. You were just born to be a kind-hearted person.

t_ch: 有点危险，躲远点。
t_en: Looks kinda dangerous. Keep your distance.

t_ch: 君子不立危墙之下，你连连后退了几步，准备避开可能的坠物风险。
t_en: An ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure. Taking several steps back, you get ready to dodge any falling hazards.

t_ch: 对了，星际和平公司有面向无名客的保险吗？你开始为自己的人身安全精打细算。
t_en: Oh yeah — wonder if the IPC pays out insurance claims for the Nameless? You start plotting out a meticulous plan for your own personal safety.

t_ch: 你在杂物箱附近驻足片刻，有声音从背后传来——
t_en: Pausing for a moment beside the stacked boxes, you hear a voice behind you...

t_ch: 一道可爱的身影出现在你身后。
t_en: A cute figure appears behind you.

t_ch: 不许动！
t_en: Freeze!

t_ch: 说的就是你，不许搞乱我的箱子！这都是我辛辛苦苦堆出来的！
t_en: Yes, you! Don't mess with my boxes! I've been painstakingly stacking them all up!

t_ch: 我又不是在搞破坏……
t_en: I'm not here to mess with anything...

t_ch: 是吗？可能是我看错了吧。
t_en: Is that so? Perhaps I'm mistaken, then.

t_ch: 以你的身高，你是怎么做到的？
t_en: How do you manage that with your height?

t_ch: 住口，不许质疑本天才的完美之躯！
t_en: Silence. Question not the bodily perfection of this here genius!

t_ch: 我记得你，大个子{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，你来得正好，本天才有一项伟大的计划准备实施，正好需要你这样的大个子人才！
t_en: I remember you, tall one. You came just in time. I've got a fantastic plan to put into place, and I could use some tall talent like yourself helping out!

t_ch: 你可以帮我把「王座」搬过来吗？
t_en: Can you help me move the "throne"?

t_ch: 那是什么？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 那是见证我荣光时刻的必需品！哇咔咔！
t_en: That's an essential item to helping others witness my glorious triumph! Mwahaha!

t_ch: 呃，我不认识你。
t_en: Uh, but I don't know you.

t_ch: 哇咔咔？你连本天才都不记得啦？那个…我的曾用名是…「芝士流心」！
t_en: Mwahaha? You forgot about a pure genius, like me? Well then... people used to call me... "Molten Cheese Tart"!

t_ch: 我不管，你是辅佐本天才的最佳人选，是你把本天才丢到这寥无人烟的地方，你得负起责任！
t_en: I don't care. You're the best choice out there for helping me because you're the one who banished me to this barren wasteland — so take some responsibility!

t_ch: 我的要求很简单，我想让你帮我把「王座」搬过来！放在这个顶上！
t_en: What I want is very simple: I'd like you to help me move the "throne" over! Up here, on top of this!

t_ch: 「王座」究竟是啥？
t_en: What exactly do you mean by the "throne"?

t_ch: 「王座」在哪儿？
t_en: Where is this "throne"?

t_ch: 别急，我带你过去，你就知道是什么东西了。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll take you there, and you'll know soon enough what it is.

t_ch: 它将你带到了它心心念念的「王座」面前。
t_en: It brings you to the throne it adores.

t_ch: 不是很重，帮我搬一下吧？拜托了！
t_en: It's not very heavy. Can you please help me move it? Please!

t_ch: 走走走！
t_en: C'mon, let's go!

t_ch: 就是这里，帮我放到顶上吧！
t_en: Here it is. Help me pop it right up the top there!

t_ch: 你将它的「王座」放到了杂物堆顶上。
t_en: You place the throne atop the stack of boxes.

t_ch: 太好了，哇咔咔，本天才可以上去了…不过……
t_en: Marvelous. Mwahaha... I can go up now... But...

t_ch: …有点太高了…我够不着……
t_en: ...It's still a little high... I can't quite reach...

t_ch: 你能把我扶上去吗？
t_en: Could you help me up?

t_ch: 你正准备答应，但在开口前的一瞬，你忽然意识到了什么。
t_en: Just as you're about to open your mouth to respond, you come to a sudden realization.

t_ch: 为什么要任由这个小家伙差遣你？这是你亲手搭建的「王座」，它的归属权在你，谁坐上它——应当由你决定！
t_en: Why are you running errands for this little thing? This is the throne that you built with your own hands. It belongs to you — and whosoever may sit on it should be decided by you and you alone!

t_ch: 坐上王座的人是谁？你认为——
t_en: Who should sit on the throne? You decide...

t_ch: 必须是我！
t_en: It has to be me!

t_ch: 就送它上去玩玩吧。
t_en: Send it on up there — just for a laugh.

t_ch: 你亲手缔造的王座，当然该由你来坐。还有比你更合适的人选吗？
t_en: Of course, the very throne you created should be sat upon by thyself. How could there be anyone more suitable?

t_ch: 当然没有！
t_en: Of course there isn't!

t_ch: 在芝士流心，哦不，现在应当叫它「洞悉者」了——在它的无奈中，你开始向上攀登。
t_en: Notwithstanding the exasperation of Molten Cheese Tart — no, it's the "Insighter" now — you start your upward climb.

t_ch: 你坐上了属于你的王座，只不过，这脆弱的空箱子不堪重负，开始摇摇晃晃。
t_en: You sit on the throne — which belongs to you — but the fragile, empty boxes cannot take the weight and they begin to shake.

t_ch: 杂物堆抖动了几下，王座和你都坠落大地。
t_en: The stack of boxes shudders several times, before the throne and you both fall to the ground.

t_ch: 你没事吧？！我都说了这玩意是为我度身定做的，你怎么不听呢。
t_en: Are you okay!? I kept telling you but you wouldn't listen — this thing was tailor-made for me.

t_ch: 让本天才检查检查…咔咔，你有没有摔伤哪里，没看到伤口，不过要是头晕的话记得去上面找那些科员检查！
t_en: Let me have a look with my genius eyes... Mwahaha, I can't find any injuries from the fall, but if you feel dizzy, please go and see a researcher for a check-up!

t_ch: 它没有责怪你，反而第一时间来关心你，这令你有点不知所措。
t_en: Instead of blaming you, its first reaction was to take care of you. This leaves you feeling a little overwhelmed.

t_ch: 你会后悔抢夺了它的王座吗？你脑子有些混乱，暂时想不明白，但你觉得以后要好好爱护它和它的同类。
t_en: Will you come to regret snatching the throne? Feeling a little discombobulated, you can't put your finger on it right now — but you feel as though you should take special care of this creation and others like it in the future.

t_ch: 你亲手缔造的王座，在你的搀扶下，你将可爱的芝士流心——哦不，现在是尊敬的洞悉者——送了上去。
t_en: With your support, approaching the throne that you created with your bare hands, you thrust up the cute Molten Cheese Tart — ah, no, it's the esteemed Insighter, now.

t_ch: 洞悉者坐上了它心念念的王座，高兴不已。王座有些摇晃，但你及时扶稳了它。
t_en: The Insighter sits atop the throne it adores, ecstatic. The throne shudders a little, but you manage to stabilize it in time.

t_ch: 在确保它不会掉下来后，你后退几步。
t_en: Once you're sure it isn't going to topple over, you take a few steps back.

t_ch: 尽管在现场见证了「无限荣光」的人只有你和它——严格来说只有你，但你还是忍不住发出了轻轻的笑声。
t_en: Despite you and the Insighter themselves being the only ones present to witness this moment of limitless glory — well, strictly speaking, it's just YOU — it's impossible to stop a chuckle from slipping out.

t_ch: 恍惚间，你觉得这种胡闹的感觉——似乎也不坏。
t_en: In a daze, you start to wonder if this kind of mischief isn't so bad after all.

t_ch: 一只路过的虫子挡住了你的去路，你注意到有微弱的声音从它的口器中传来——
t_en: An insect passing by blocks your path. You notice a faint sound coming out from its mandibles...

t_ch: 「救命呀！」会是虫子在求救吗？显然不是，这究竟是谁呢，你开始思索……
t_en: "Save me!" Is this insect asking for help? Obviously not. So, who could this be? You start to ponder deeply...

t_ch: 虫子发现了你！
t_en: The insect notices you!

t_ch: 呃，我有点晕，抱歉…刚刚是你救了我吗？
t_en: Uh, I'm feeling a little dizzy. Sorry... Are you the one who just saved me?

t_ch: 可能是吧。
t_en: Maybe.

t_ch: 这里只有我和你，肯定是你。
t_en: It's just you and me here. It has to be you.

t_ch: 没错，就是在下！
t_en: That's right. I'm the one!

t_ch: 谢谢你，好心人。
t_en: Thank you, good-hearted stranger.

t_ch: …我感觉好恶心。
t_en: ...I feel sick.

t_ch: 呃，我不是在说你，我在那家伙胃里待久了，身上还有胃液的气味，好恶心……
t_en: Er, I didn't mean I'm sick of you. I was stuck in that bug's stomach for ages and I still reek of gastric juices. So, so gross...

t_ch: 你被虫子吃了？
t_en: Were you gobbled up by an insect?

t_ch: 算是吧，但不太对，因为那家伙压根没咀嚼，就直接把我吞到它肚子里了。
t_en: Pretty much. But also not quite right, because that bug didn't even bother to chew me up before swallowing me down its belly.

t_ch: 你没被它消化掉吗？
t_en: How'd you avoid getting digested?

t_ch: 这个…好像是我头顶的「馅料」成分有问题，简单来说，它好像对我的身体有点过敏，没办法消化我的表皮。
t_en: Well... I think it's something to do with the stuffing ingredients on my head. Basically, the monster seems to be slightly allergic to me and so it can't digest my skin.

t_ch: 晕…晕乎乎的，好恶心……
t_en: Dizzy... I'm so dizzy. I feel sick...

t_ch: 抱歉，我身上不干净，我得找个地方清理一下了，这点小礼物就送给你吧。
t_en: Sorry, I'm filthy. I need to find a place to wash up. Here's a little something for you.

t_ch: （为它指引回家的路）
t_en: (Point out the way home for it)

t_ch: 哇，我终于不用再流浪了！谢谢你，{F#漂亮大姐姐}{M#帅气的大哥哥}。
t_en: Wow, I won't have to keep stumbling around! Thank you, {F#lovely miss}{M#good sir}.

t_ch: 在你的指引下，小家伙回到了它温暖的新家。
t_en: With your guidance, the little creation made it back to its cozy new home.

t_ch: 所以说你的这版方案就是天方夜谭，根本无法满足需求......
t_en: That is precisely why your plan is pure nonsense, these requirements simply cannot be met...

t_ch: 我怎么就...喂，我们在谈论正事，闲杂人等不要添乱。
t_en: How can I... Um, we are discussing formal affairs, we do not need unnecessary interference.

t_ch: 等等，我认得这个人，{F#她}{M#他}、{F#她}{M#他}就是那位贝洛伯格的大救星！
t_en: Wait, I know this person, {F#Sh—She}{M#H—He} is the savior of Belobog!

t_ch: 哦！你就是大名鼎鼎的{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Oh! You're the famous {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 这下好了！我们这儿正有些拿不定主意的东西，需要听听你的意见。
t_en: This is perfect! We've got a few things here that we just can't settle, we need your opinion.

t_ch: 你应该知道，如今贝洛伯格面临的最大挑战就是修复这台造物引擎——但这个项目具体该怎么推行，大家始终没能统一思想。
t_en: I'm sure you know, the biggest challenge facing Belobog right now is repairing this Engine of Creation... However, none of us can seem to agree on how to move this project forward.

t_ch: 现在我们各有各的方案，谁也说服不了谁。
t_en: We've all got our different plans at the moment, and none of us can convince the others.

t_ch: 你去过那么多地方，肯定见多识广。你的意见，我们听。
t_en: You've been to so many places, I'm sure you must be experienced and knowledgeable. We will listen to your insights.

t_ch: 所以，我们打算把各自的想法集中到一起，由你选出在你看来最恰当的提案——当然，你自己有什么想法也可以提。
t_en: So, we're going to put our ideas together, and you can select the plan you feel the most appropriate... Of course, if you have your own ideas, you can suggest them, too.

t_ch: 大家都没有疑问吧？
t_en: No one has any objections, do they?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No!

t_ch: 给我看看你们的提案。
t_en: Let me see your plans.

t_ch: 我还有些其他事要处理。
t_en: I have other things to do.

t_ch: 行，反正我们几个一直在这，等什么时候你有空了再来。
t_en: Very well. We will be here, so come back when you have time.

t_ch: 你回来了，现在有空拿主意了吗？
t_en: You're back! Do you have time to make a decision now?

t_ch: 给我看看你们的提案。
t_en: Let me see your plans.

t_ch: 好极了，我们开始吧。
t_en: Great, let's begin.

t_ch: 我还没准备好。
t_en: I'm not ready yet.

t_ch: 行，反正我们几个一直在这，等什么时候你有空了再来。
t_en: Very well. We will be here, so come back when you have time.

t_ch: 那么，第一个议题：造物引擎的双臂应该安装什么？
t_en: Alright, the first agenda item: What should the Engine of Creation have installed on its arms?

t_ch: 五指手臂。
t_en: A hand with five fingers.

t_ch: 这种机械臂拥有和人类相同的五根手指，可以持握各种装备，是最能适应复杂工作的结构。
t_en: If a robot like this has five fingers like a human, it can hold various kinds of equipment which will allow it to be a suitable apparatus for complex tasks.

t_ch: 但是五指能怎么操作，难道只靠一根控制杆吗？驾驶员会头疼的。
t_en: But how will the five fingers be operated? Surely not with a single joystick? The driver will end up going insane!

t_ch: 别忘了，「公司」说过会与我们分享技术。他们的机兵肯定早就解决了这个问题。
t_en: Don't forget that the IPC said that they will share their technology with us. Their automatons must have solved this issue a long time ago.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 明智的选择。之后我会去和「公司」的外派员工请教。
t_en: A wise decision! I will go to an IPC worker and ask them for advice.

t_ch: 哼，希望「公司」真能有这么好心。
t_en: Humph, let's hope the IPC is feeling generous.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 爪钩手臂。
t_en: An arm with a clawed hand.

t_ch: 这种手臂由上下两部分组成，就像一个巨大的钳子。它只能从事简单的抓取工作，无法适应复杂环境。
t_en: This kind of hand would consist of an upper and lower part, like a giant pincer. It would only be able to complete simple acts like grabbing and lifting, it would not be suited for intricate tasks.

t_ch: 但它不用看完砖一样厚的说明书，知道怎么按按钮就可以操作。
t_en: However, you wouldn't have to read an encyclopedia-sized instruction manual to know what buttons to press to control whatever.

t_ch: 在未来的重建工作中，造物引擎的规模和数量是最重要的。有了它，驾驶员的培训压力会低很多。
t_en: In future reconstruction work, the number and scale of the Engines of Creation will be of utmost importance. With this design, the pressure on training the operators will be greatly relieved.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 很好，这下驾驶员的招聘标准可以放宽些了。
t_en: Very good. Now we can relax the recruitment criteria for operators slightly.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 伐木机枪。
t_en: A tree-felling machine gun.

t_ch: 伐木为什么要用机枪？
t_en: Why would you need a machine gun for tree-felling?

t_ch: 不用枪，难不成一棵树一棵树去锯？
t_en: You think sawing them down one-by-one is better than just using a gun?

t_ch: 这种机枪既是武器又是工具，一轮扫射下去，整片森林都会变成木材——重建工作肯定需要木材。
t_en: A machine gun like this is both a weapon and a tool. With one spray of this gun, an entire forest becomes timber... Reconstruction naturally needs timber.

t_ch: 可是贝洛伯格哪来的树？
t_en: And where will Belobog be getting these trees from?

t_ch: …将来会有的。
t_en: ...There will be trees here in the future.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 你看你看，{NICKNAME}也同意了。我早就说过这个方案是可行的。造物引擎很快就要脱胎换骨了！
t_en: Look, look! {NICKNAME} agrees! I knew my plan could work! The Engine of Creation is about to become a whole new version of itself!
Ahhh...

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 采矿钻。
t_en: A mining drill.

t_ch: 这是一种螺旋状的金属钻头，可以开采地表下的地髓或其他矿石，也可以用于挖掘隧道、打通山峰。
t_en: This is a spiral-shaped metal drill bit, it can be used to collect underground Geomarrow and other ores, as well as dig tunnels to allow for passage through the mountains.

t_ch: 没错~有了它，我们就能建立更多矿场和铁路，把星球各处的资源全部握到手里。
t_en: That's right! With this, we can create more mines and railways, bringing all this planet's resources straight into our hands.

t_ch: 之后不管是卖给「公司」还是拿来自用，都会比现在富裕多了。
t_en: Whether we end up selling them to the IPC or using them ourselves, we will end up far richer than we are now!

t_ch: 但资源终有采空的一天，不是吗？
t_en: But there will come a day when our resources are exhausted, will there not?

t_ch: 那都是不知多久以后的事了，还是先操心当下吧。
t_en: That's a problem for who knows when! We should worry about the present for now.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 玩转星际的无名客果然有眼光。
t_en: I knew you'd have good taste, nameless wanderer of the universe.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 礼花炮。
t_en: A firework cannon.

t_ch: 那是...什么？
t_en: What... is that?

t_ch: 我听说在旧世界，人们会把花瓣装进加压容器里，在举办庆典时引爆。
t_en: I heard that in the Old World, people would place flower petals into a pressurized vessel and blow them up at festivals.

t_ch: 然后五颜六色的碎屑会漫天飞舞，就像雨雪或繁星。
t_en: Then, multicolored debris would dance up into the sky, like snow or stars.

t_ch: 听起来好浪漫......
t_en: That sounds quite romantic...

t_ch: 是吗？它能用来干什么呢？
t_en: You think? What could that be used for?

t_ch: 当然是在庆祝时增添气氛啦，为什么造物引擎就不能拿来做娱乐工具呢？现在这个时候，希望才是人们最需要的东西。
t_en: To liven up the party at a celebration, of course! Why can't the Engine of Creation be a tool for recreation and fun? In times like this, hope is the most important thing to us all.

t_ch: 也...不是没有道理。
t_en: You're not... entirely wrong.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 开拓者的眼界果然与众不同——不如我们这就准备去办个庆典吧。
t_en: I knew you saw things differently, Trailblazer. Let's prepare a celebration!

t_ch: 别急，方案还没讨论完呢。
t_en: Hold on, we haven't finished discussing the plans.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 我们来看下一个议题：造物引擎的下身应该安装什么？
t_en: Let's look at the next item on the agenda: What should the Engine of Creation have installed on its lower body?

t_ch: 履带。
t_en: Caterpillar tracks.

t_ch: 古老且传统的设计，不仅越野性能强大，而且还能够降低机体重心、避免因出力导致的侧翻和后仰。
t_en: An old and traditional design that not only allows it to perform well off-road, it can also lower the body's center of gravity to prevent rollover and backwards lean when using power.

t_ch: 考虑到造物引擎可能承担的重型劳动，我认为这个方案是最为合适的。
t_en: Considering the heavy labor that the Engine of Creation will likely be taking on, I believe that this plan is most suitable.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 这方面大家倒是没什么异议。
t_en: We actually have no objections to this idea.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 双腿。
t_en: Two legs.

t_ch: 考虑到之前造物引擎就是使用双腿行走，我们还是按照原本的样子修复它更好些。
t_en: Considering that the Engine of Creation once walked on two legs, I think we should recreate it according to the original design.

t_ch: 其他方案都会耗费额外成本，而且可能会出现意想不到的问题。
t_en: Other plans will cost extra expenses and may even lead to unforeseen problems.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 保守的选择，但确实不容易出错。
t_en: A safe choice, but it is indeed unlikely to cause problems.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 反重力引擎。
t_en: An anti-grav engine.

t_ch: 我认为传统的移动手段存在一个致命问题：它们无法应对高山和峡谷。
t_en: I believe there is a fatal flaw in traditional methods of movement: They cannot overcome mountains and valleys.

t_ch: 这颗星球上最不缺的就是极端地形。一旦遇到常规手段无法翻越的区域，造物引擎就会寸步难行。
t_en: One thing that this planet has in excess is extreme terrain. As soon as it reaches an area that traditional methods of traversal are insufficient, the Engine of Creation will be stopped in its tracks.

t_ch: 所以我打算直接让造物引擎飞起来——别说不可能实现，「公司」肯定有这种技术！只要他们提供帮助，一切问题都可以解决。
t_en: So, I plan to let the Engine of Creation take to the sky! Don't tell me it cannot be done, the IPC definitely has this technology! As long as they are willing to help, any problem can be solved!

t_ch: 他们真有吗......
t_en: Do they really have that...?

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 很好，等一切结束后，我们的征途就会是星辰蓝天。
t_en: Wonderful! When this is all over, our road ahead will lead straight to the stars!

t_ch: 最好别把目光放那么远，不然你会受不了现在的生活。
t_en: It's best not to crave the future so deeply, or you will only suffer the present.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 四足。
t_en: Four legs.

t_ch: 呃，其实这方面我没什么特别的想法，就是觉得四足看起来像迷你机兵...多可爱呀。
t_en: Uhh, I actually don't have any real thoughts about this, I just thought they would look like mini automatons with four legs... How cute would that be?

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 哇哦，这...同道中人？
t_en: Wow, you... you agree with me?

t_ch: 看来「造物机兵」这名字也不错。
t_en: Looks like the "Automaton of Creation" would make a pretty fitting name.

t_ch: 你认真的吗...
t_en: Are you serious...?

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 螺旋桨。
t_en: A propeller.

t_ch: 这...我们星球全是陆地啊，您为什么提出这个方案？
t_en: That's... Our entire planet is land, why would you suggest that?

t_ch: 冰雪开化之后，雅利洛-Ⅵ可能确实会成为一个海洋星球。
t_en: Once the ice and snow melts, Jarilo-VI could become an oceanic world.

t_ch: 我在星球仪上看到过，在过去的某个时候，这颗星球上的确是有海的。我们的祖先曾经乘船环游世界，不知什么时候轮到咱们。
t_en: I saw this on the armillary sphere before, this planet did indeed have oceans at one point in the past. Our ancestors once traveled the world on ships, I wonder when it will be our turn to do the same.

t_ch: 哇哦，这么说{NICKNAME}已经开始考虑几百年后的事了...这就是远见吗？！
t_en: Wow, you mean that you've already begun considering life hundreds of years in the future, {NICKNAME}... are you really that forward-thinking!?

t_ch: 怎么你们从来没夸过我有远见？！
t_en: Why do you never praise me for being forward-thinking!?

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 的确，我们不能只着眼当下，完全不考虑未来。
t_en: Indeed, we cannot be blinded by the present and not consider the future at all.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}，你让我找回了造物之柱工程的初心。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}, you've made me regain my initial hopes for the Pillars of Creation project.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 接下来的议题是...造物引擎的背上应该安装哪种辅助配件？
t_en: The next item on the agenda is... What kind of auxiliary equipment should the Engines of Creation have installed on their backs?

t_ch: 机兵加工厂。
t_en: An automaton processing factory.

t_ch: 到那时候，机兵就不再需要武器了。它们会参与更多劳动，以工人而非战士的身份与我们共存。
t_en: When that time comes, automatons will no longer need weapons. They will join in with labor and live alongside us as not warriors, but workers.

t_ch: 假如造物引擎可以承担机兵的装配工作，我们就能把它当作一个移动基地，随时为矿场和其他生产设施提供支持。
t_en: If the Engines of Creation were able to take on the work of automaton assembly, we could use them as mobile bases that could provide support to mines or other production facilities at any time.

t_ch: 可是要让造物引擎自己生产机兵的话，会带来很多额外开销吧......
t_en: But if we will have Engines of Creation creating automatons by themselves, that will bring a lot of extra expenses, surely...

t_ch: 那就看{NICKNAME}如何决定了。
t_en: Let's see what {NICKNAME} decides.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 嗯...这样的话，我得联系矿场那边多备些矿石了。
t_en: Hmm... if that's the case, I'll have to contact the mines and have them prepare more ore.

t_ch: 这钱花得也算值，不是吗？
t_en: The money will be worth it though, don't you think?

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 塔吊。
t_en: A tower crane.

t_ch: 考虑到未来我们可能需要修缮更多的造物引擎…我认为这第一台成品应该起到一点带头作用。
t_en: Thinking of the future, we will likely need to repair more Engines of Creation in the future... I believe that the first finished product should serve as the vanguard in this.

t_ch: 如果装上高出力的塔吊，它就能协助我们修复其他机体，凑出一支由造物引擎组成的「筑城队」。
t_en: If we equip a powerful tower crane, it will be able to assist us with repairing other machines and form a "fortification team" made up of Engines of Creation.

t_ch: 而且，贝洛伯格不该是我们唯一的家园。等到星球环境恢复后，我们有必要建造其他的城市，届时一台安装了塔吊的造物引擎肯定会帮上大忙。
t_en: What's more, Belobog cannot be our only home. Once the planet's environment has recovered, we will need to create other cities. When that time comes, an Engine of Creation equipped with a tower crane is sure to be a huge help.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 遍布星球各地的同胞...真想亲眼看见那一天啊。
t_en: Our kin-folk spread across an entire planet... I long to see such a day with my own eyes.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 能量护盾。
t_en: An energy shield.

t_ch: 杰作，我只能这样评价。还有什么比一面覆盖了整台机器的护盾更令人安心呢？
t_en: It can only be described as a masterpiece. What could bring us more relief than a shield that completely covers the entire machine?

t_ch: 这项技术已经在机兵「甲虫」身上得到了验证。我们只需要把它放大，然后装在造物引擎的背上就好了。
t_en: This technology has already been tested on the automaton "Beetles." We just need to enlarge it and equip it to the back of the Engines of Creation.

t_ch: 为什么我们会需要一面巨大的护盾？
t_en: Why would they need a giant shield?

t_ch: 问得好——因为球外势力的威胁已经大到不能忽视了。
t_en: A good question! Because the extraterrestrial threats to our planet are already too great to be ignored.

t_ch: 这次是「公司」，下次又会是什么？哪怕没法反击，我们也要有自保的能力才行。
t_en: For now, it's the IPC, but what will it be next time? Even if we cannot fight back, we must be able to protect ourselves.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 完美，还有什么比一座移动要塞更酷炫的吗？！
t_en: Perfect! What could be cooler than a moving fortress!?

t_ch: 等你给这座「要塞」补充能量时就笑不出来了。
t_en: You won't be laughing when you have to charge this "fortress."

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 农业设备。
t_en: Agricultural equipment.

t_ch: 准确的说，是圆盘犁、播种机和喷水壶的三合一装置。
t_en: To be more precise, a three-part combination of a disk plow, a planter, and a watering system.

t_ch: 想获利，先种地。等到环境恢复之后，外面肯定有大块的土地等待我们耕作。
t_en: If you want to reap rewards, you must first plant seeds. When the environment recovers, there is sure to be a large amount of land out there, waiting for us to cultivate.

t_ch: 只要有了这一系列设备，造物引擎就能不断开辟疆域、拓展边界，它们脚下的大地皆是贝洛伯格的沃土。
t_en: If we have this set of equipment, the Engines of Creation will be able to keep opening up new territory and expanding our borders. Every bit of earth they tread upon will become a part of Belobog.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 终有一日，贝洛伯格将不再是一座孤独之城。
t_en: One day, Belobog will no longer be a lonely city.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 灯光球。
t_en: A disco ball.

t_ch: 我知道这个！在过去，人们会用这种灯来给舞会照明，甚至做出美妙的色彩变化。
t_en: I know this one! In the past, people would use them to illuminate parties. They even change color beautifully!

t_ch: 舞会嘛...民众的娱乐需求的确重要，但我们真有场地需要这么大的灯来照亮？
t_en: Parties, huh... Though the recreational needs of the people are important, do we really have a venue that would require a giant light like that?

t_ch: 我们可以向公司或其他天外来客出租。最近我在手机上刷到一个概念叫...巡演！对，这种东西一听就很有搞头。
t_en: We can rent it out to the IPC and other extraterrestrial guests! I recently came across a new concept on my phone, it's called going on... going on tour! Doesn't hearing something like that just make your ears perk up?

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 与星际接轨是好事，只是...我们这些老家伙估计难免落伍啊。
t_en: Connecting with the universe is a great thing, it's just... It's hard for us old folks to not feel behind the times.

t_ch: 这么说，你没参加过舞会？
t_en: Are you saying, you've never been to a party before?

t_ch: 当时只顾着读书...咳，这些闲话日后再说吧。
t_en: When I was young, all my efforts went on studying... Ahem. We can save this idle chit-chat for another time.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 接下来是最后一个议题：动力核心的燃料应该采用哪种能源？
t_en: Next, we have our final item: What fuel source should be used for the power cores?

t_ch: 说实话，这门技术我们讨论的不多，目前只有两种方案。如果你有什么更好的见解，尽管说吧。
t_en: To be frank, we have not spoken a great deal about this technology and only have two plans. If you have any better ideas, please do say.

t_ch: 地髓萃取液。
t_en: Geomarrow extract.

t_ch: 对于造物引擎这样庞大的机器来说，强劲的动力是最重要的。液化提纯后的地髓蕴含着极大的能量，足以在短时间内满足造物引擎的高功率消耗。
t_en: For a giant machine like the Engine of Creation, power is absolutely crucial. Geomarrow that has been purified and liquidized contains a great amount of energy that can meet the high power consumption of the Engine of Creation over a short time.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 这种能源必须经由多道程序加工，可能会花不少功夫...好在需求的量级不大。
t_en: An energy source like this has to undergo various stages of refining and will take a lot of effort... Fortunately, the volume required will not be as great.

t_ch: 如果公司愿意帮忙的话，或许很快就可以凑齐。
t_en: If the IPC is willing to help, we may be able to prepare it in no time.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 地髓结晶颗粒。
t_en: Geomarrow crystal kernels.

t_ch: 对于造物引擎这样庞大的机器来说，持久的动力是最重要的。地髓结晶可以维持极长时间的燃烧，从而为造物引擎提供源源不断的能量——就是需求的量级可能有点大...
t_en: For a giant machine like the Engine of Creation, having power that lasts is crucial. Geomarrow crystals can sustain combustion for a long time, allowing them to provide endless energy to the Engine of Creation. The only problem is that the quantity needed will be considerable...

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 这种地髓矿不需要精细加工，应该可以很快凑齐。
t_en: This kind of Geomarrow ore does not require intricate refining processes. We should be able to gather it in no time.

t_ch: 的确，这让我们完工的速度又快了一些。
t_en: Indeed, this will reduce our time till completion even further.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 水洗煤。
t_en: Washed coal.

t_ch: 那是什么？听起来不像能用来给造物引擎供能的样子。
t_en: What's that? Doesn't sound like something that could power an Engine of Creation.

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 爱。
t_en: Love.

t_ch: 爱...是一种能源？
t_en: Love... is a source of energy?

t_ch: 不要小看这个提案。我听过一个说法：爱是宇宙中最强大的力量，能超越时间和空间......
t_en: Don't underestimate this suggestion. I've heard a saying before: "Love is the greatest power in the universe. It can transcend time and space..."

t_ch: 说不定，它其实是源自某位「星神」的力量。
t_en: Perhaps it is a power that comes from a certain Aeon.

t_ch: 就它了。
t_en: This one.

t_ch: 用最强大的力量来为造物引擎提供动力...听起来很合理。
t_en: Using the most powerful source of energy to power the Engine of Creation... That sounds logical.

t_ch: 不过，我们要怎么利用这种力量呢......
t_en: Although, how are we supposed to harness this energy...?

t_ch: 之后去找大守护者问问吧......
t_en: Let's go and ask the Supreme Guardian later...

t_ch: 再想想。
t_en: Let's keep thinking.

t_ch: 好，关于造物引擎的所有议题都结束了，我们总算得出了一份方案。
t_en: Alright, discussions on all the Engine of Creation proposals are complete, and we have finally found a plan.

t_ch: 不过...因为这项工程很大程度上要倚仗「公司」，我们四个是没有权力做决策的。
t_en: Although... this project will rely heavily on the IPC, so the four of us will have no right to make the final decisions.

t_ch: 之后我会把它转交给托帕总监，今天的讨论多谢你主持大局了。
t_en: I will hand these over to Director Topaz. Thank you for overseeing the discussion today.

t_ch: 可惜工地上没什么值钱的东西，就先收下这些吧。
t_en: It's a shame we have nothing of value on the site. Take these as our thanks.

t_ch: 几个木箱子凌乱地摆在这儿，里面不知装有什么宝贝。
t_en: A few wooden boxes are piled carelessly on the floor. Who knows what treasures they hold.

t_ch: 打开看看。
t_en: Open them up.

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 你掀开箱盖，成堆的零件和钢板映入眼帘。
t_en: You open the crate to find a pile of parts and steel plates.

t_ch: ...比起宝贝，它看起来更像施工剩下的废料。
t_en: ...Looks more like construction scrap than treasure.

t_ch: 哟，没想到有心人不止我一个。
t_en: Oh! I see it is not just me that cares for these things.

t_ch: 一个男人悄悄走到你身边。
t_en: A man quietly walks up to your side.

t_ch: 这些工地上的废料，过不了多久就要被统一销毁。真是浪费！
t_en: Scrap like this from construction sites will just end up being destroyed. It's such a waste!

t_ch: 如果造物工程一直这样大手大脚，过不了几年就揭不开锅了。
t_en: If they continue to be so wasteful with this project, it won't be long before they bust!

t_ch: 除了这些，工地上还有其他三处木箱。只要你把里面的东西都带给我，我就可以付你报酬。
t_en: There are three other areas with crates on this site. If you can bring the items inside to me, I will reward you.

t_ch: 为什么你会想要这些废料？
t_en: Why would you want scrap like this?

t_ch: ...一个人的垃圾，另一个人的财宝——多简单的道理。
t_en: ...One man's trash is another's treasure. Everyone knows that.

t_ch: 哪怕是从造物引擎身上掉下来的废铁，至少也具备文化价值。比起白白扔掉，拿去做成纪念品不是更好？
t_en: Even if it's just scrap metal that's fallen from the body of the Engine of Creation, it still has cultural value! Don't you think it would be better to use them as souvenirs than cast them out as scrap?

t_ch: 等你拿完东西后，就去桥对岸的设备旁找我。眼睛放亮点，别被人跟踪了。
t_en: When you're done collecting them, come and find me by the equipment on the other side of the bridge. Keep your eyes peeled and make sure you're not followed.

t_ch: 几个木箱子凌乱地摆在这儿，看来它就是第二处废料点。
t_en: A few wooden boxes are piled carelessly on the floor. Looks like this is the second scrap point.

t_ch: 取走里面的东西。
t_en: Take the items inside.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 你掀开箱盖，里面是随手堆放的信号灯和金属外壳。
t_en: You open the crate to find a messy pile of signal lights and metal casings.

t_ch: 几个木箱子凌乱地摆在这儿，看来它就是第三处废料点。
t_en: A few wooden boxes are piled carelessly on the floor. Looks like this is the third scrap point.

t_ch: 取走里面的东西。
t_en: Take the items inside.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 你掀开箱盖，里面是零散的铁脚手架和螺丝。
t_en: You open the crate to find scattered iron scaffolds and screws.

t_ch: 造物之柱工程的材料量果然庞大，连这种废品都装了几箱子。
t_en: The number of materials needed for the Pillars of Creation project really is immense. Even scrap like this takes up multiple boxes.

t_ch: 几个木箱子凌乱地摆在这儿，看来它就是最后一处废料点。
t_en: A few wooden boxes are piled carelessly on the floor. Looks like this is the last scrap point.

t_ch: 取走里面的东西。
t_en: Take the items inside.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 你掀开箱盖，里面是略有锈迹的轴承和钢条。
t_en: You open the crate to find slightly rusted bearings and steel bars.
